questions.question: Why is the K-Nearest Neighbors algorithm often referred to as 'instance-based' or 'lazy' learning? (2 marks)
keyPoints: It does not construct an explicit general model during the training phase.
It simply memorizes (stores) all the training instances.
All computation and generalization is deferred until the prediction phase when a query is made.

questions.question: Discuss the advantages and disadvantages of the K-Nearest Neighbors algorithm. Suggest one method to address a key disadvantage you mention. (5 marks)
keyPoints: Advantage: Simple to understand and implement with no explicit training phase.
Advantage: Naturally handles multi-class classification problems.
Disadvantage: Computationally expensive at prediction time (must calculate distances to all points).
Disadvantage: Sensitive to irrelevant features - suffers from the 'curse of dimensionality'.
Disadvantage: Performance is highly dependent on the choice of K and the distance metric.
Mitigation Suggestion: To address computational expense, use efficient data structures like KD-Trees or Ball Trees for neighbor searches.
